items.title: Tabung Haji RCI report delay: Anwar cites confidence concerns
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government did not release the Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) report on Tabung Haji three years ago because doing so at the time could have triggered a crisis of confidence that might have led to the institution's collapse. Speaking on the issue, Anwar said the government believed that publishing the report then could have caused depositors to lose confidence in Tabung Haji and withdraw their savings, threatening the viability of the pilgrimage fund. I said that three years ago, we could not have made it public.

items.title: Johor police arrest three, seize 71 bitcoin miners in TNB power theft bust
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor police have busted a syndicate illegally mining Bitcoin using electricity stolen from Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB), following four raids on July 22 and 23. Police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the operation, codenamed Op Letrik, was carried out by the Johor police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department (D4), working alongside TNB's Southern Region SEAL team. During a press conference at the Johor police headquarters today, he said the items seized included 71 cryptocurrency mining machines, two computers, two laptops, five routers, two monitors, two computer keyboards, a mobile phone and two vehicles.

items.title: UK retail sales unexpectedly rise 1pct in June
items.summary: LONDON: British retail sales volumes unexpectedly rose by 1.0 per cent in monthly terms in June, according to official figures published on Friday. Economists polled by Reuters had mostly expected a monthly fall of 0.3 per cent in sales volumes. Compared with a year earlier, volumes were 4.2 per cent higher, against economists' expectations of a 2.3 per cent rise.

items.title: N9 early polling centres to be closed in stages
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Early polling centres for the 16th Negri Sembilan state election, which open at 8am on July 28, will be closed in stages between noon and 5pm. According to a Federal Government Gazette dated July 23 regarding the polling hours for the state election, 18 out of 38 early polling centres will close at 12pm. Early polling centres in Bahau and one in Paroi will close at 2pm, while 18 others will close at 5pm.

items.title: Boy, 5, dies with bruises; mother and boyfriend held
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A 5-year-old boy was found dead with bruises on his body at a rented apartment in Gelugor, prompting police to arrest his mother and her boyfriend. The Indonesian woman, 30, and her Bangladeshi boyfriend, 34, who told police they were husband and wife, were later remanded for seven days to assist investigations into the child's death. Hospital staff later lodged a police report after discovering bruises across the child's body.

items.title: Chong Wei visits US Embassy ahead of Los Angeles Games preparations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Badminton legend Datuk Seri Lee Chong Wei, a three-time Olympic silver medallist and former world number one, visited the United States Embassy in Kuala Lumpur ahead of his upcoming trip to Los Angeles. Chong Wei is Badminton Association of Malaysia performance committee chairman. "It was a privilege to welcome Malaysian badminton legend Datuk Seri Lee Chong Wei to the US Embassy," the embassy said in its post, adding that few athletes have inspired more Malaysians through sport than Lee.

items.title: Hannah Yeoh likens BN-PN pact to Titanic sinking
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional is like the Titanic waiting to sink, DAP deputy secretary-general Hannah Yeoh said. She said the outcome was inevitable as the partnership was not built on trust or loyalty. Yeoh said both BN and PN harboured ambitions to lead the federal government.

items.title: Motorist swerves to avoid animal, plunges car into Sungai Langat
items.summary: KAJANG: A 56-year-old driver escaped unhurt after his car plunged into Sungai Langat when he swerved to avoid an animal in Hulu Langat yesterday. Kajang police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the man was driving from Sungai Congkak to Nilai when he spotted an animal crossing the road near Institut Kemahiran Belia Negara. "His attempt to avoid the animal caused him to lose control of the vehicle before it skidded and fell into Sungai Langat," Naazron said in a statement.

items.title: SK Bukit Pantai gets rebuilt classroom block after termite damage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: SK Bukit Pantai in Bangsar today received the keys to its newly rebuilt Block C, replacing the original structure that suffered extensive termite-related structural damage over several years. Also present was SK Bukit Pantai headmaster Shaharuddin Eusop. "SK Bangsar, SK Bukit Bandaraya and SK Bukit Pantai are currently operating at full capacity.

items.title: School van driver charged over sexual assault of 8-year-old
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A 60-year-old school van driver pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court to sexually assaulting an 8-year-old girl last week. Deputy Public Prosecutor Nadhirah Abdul Rahim prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Muhammad Nur Arif. The prosecution did not propose bail as the offence involved a child victim.

items.title: RM500 aid for Penang padi, vegetable farmers
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: The Penang government has approved a one-off RM500 Bantuan Sara Hidup for 6,572 eligible padi and vegetable farmers in the state, involving an allocation of RM3.29 million. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the assistance, which will benefit 5,567 padi farmers and 1,005 vegetable farmers, is part of the state government's efforts to strengthen the food security agenda. Chow said the assistance also fulfils the state government's commitment under the Penang Unity Manifesto 2023, particularly Theme 10: Food Supply Chain Security, which emphasises cost of living assistance for padi farmers, vegetable growers and fishermen.

items.title: Over 100 Rohingya families identified in Ketereh
items.summary: KOTA BARU: More than 100 Rohingya families have been identified as living at several locations in Ketereh for the past several years. Kelantan Immigration Department director Garry Belayong John Siki said Ketereh remained the largest Rohingya settlement in the state to date. He said the department had been continuously monitoring the settlement and would step up enforcement and surveillance from time to time.

items.title: 11 men charged as members of organised crime syndicate 'Vinod Gang'
items.summary: SEPANG: Eleven men were brought before the Sessions Court here today on charges of being members of an organised crime syndicate known as Vinod Gang. Earlier, all 11 accused arrived at the court compound around 9am under heavy security from the police's Criminal Investigation Department (CID). In a statement yesterday, Bukit Aman CID director Datuk M. Kumar said the group is believed to have been active since 2023 and involved in gang robberies, attempted murder, armed rioting, causing injury with weapons and mischief.

items.title: MMA urges stronger workplace protections after Sabah hospital allegations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has called for protection of complainants and witnesses, as well as strict confidentiality, during investigations into allegations of sexual harassment and workplace bullying. He said MMA viewed seriously the allegations of sexual harassment and workplace bullying involving a public hospital director in Sabah that came to light recently. Yesterday, Sabah Police Commissioner Datuk Jauteh Dikun said police were investigating allegations of sexual harassment involving the director after receiving a report in January.

items.title: BN-PN understanding is no charade, says Hadi
items.summary: MARANG: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has dismissed claims that cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional in the Negri Sembilan election is a charade. He said both coalitions had demonstrated their commitment throughout the campaign period. "The understanding between both coalitions is not a charade," he said after Friday prayers at Masjid Rusila.

items.title: Mastermind among 11 charged with being members of ‘Vinod Gang’ crime syndicate in Sepang
items.summary: SEPANG, July 24 — Eleven men were brought before the Sessions Court here today on charges of being members of an organised crime syndicate known as “Vinod Gang.” No plea was recorded after the charges were read before Judge Husna Dzulkifly, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. Earlier, all 11 accused arrived at the court compound around 9 am under heavy security from the Royal Malaysia Police’s Criminal Investigation Department (CID). In a statement yesterday, Bukit Aman CID director Datuk M Kumar said the group is believed to have been active since 2023 and involved in gang robberies, attempted murder, armed rioting, causing injury with weapons and mischief.

items.title: Teen couple plead guilty to imitation pistol, identity card charges
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: An 18-year-old couple pleaded guilty in separate cases in the Magistrate's Court here today to possessing an imitation pistol and another person's identity card. Tharmindraa admitted to possessing an imitation metal pistol at 942-H, Halaman Paya Terubong, Mukim 13, Relau, at 12.35am on July 21. Magistrate S. Selvaranjini allowed bail at RM3,000 with one surety and fixed Aug 20 for mention pending the social report.

items.title: Sabah mulls ban on endangered mameng
items.summary: Sabah Fisheries Department director Datin Dr Shuhadah Mustapha said although the species, locally known as mameng, can currently be caught, sold and consumed within Sabah, the government is willing to consider stronger conservation measures. Shuhadah said Sabah already imposes a zero export quota on the species as a conservation measure. "However, domestic trade of the Humphead Wrasse within Sabah is currently not prohibited, and the species may be caught, sold, consumed and cultured in cages," she told the New Straits Times.

items.title: Malaysia opposes Israel's actions, not Jewish people, says Anwar
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim reiterated Malaysia's stance against Zionist Israel, while emphasising that the country is not against the Jewish people. He said claims that Malaysia is racist towards a particular race are unfounded. Yesterday, Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said Malaysia had reaffirmed its longstanding policy of not recognising Israel to the United States Department of State.

items.title: Former minister Idris Jala diagnosed with stage three lung cancer
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former minister Datuk Seri Idris Jala has been diagnosed with stage three lung cancer after undergoing a medical check-up following a persistent cough. Idris, 67, revealed his diagnosis during a recent interview on his son Leon Jala's YouTube channel, The Game of Impossible, saying he would step away from co-hosting duties while his son continues hosting the programme. In 2017, Pemandu was transformed into Pemandu Associates Sdn Bhd, where Idris currently serves as president and chairman.

items.title: Hadi: Umno’s Malay supremacy and PAS’s Islamic supremacy are two sides of the same coin
items.summary: MARANG, July 24 — The concepts of “Malay supremacy” (Ketuanan Melayu) and “Islamic supremacy” (Ketuanan Islam) will not trigger an ideological clash in the political cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN). Instead, PAS President Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang claimed the two visions can be seamlessly integrated. Speaking after a Duha lecture at Rusila Mosque on Friday, Abdul Hadi argued that the fundamental principles of Malay identity and Islam are complementary and inseparable, Sinar Harian reported.

items.title: KL police keeping watch for foreign groups exploiting humanitarian causes
items.summary: Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said police would work with all relevant parties, including embassies, to tackle the problem. Fadil said the post would serve as a central staging area for operations, while improving coordination between police, auxiliary police, security teams and other stakeholders. He said Kuala Lumpur currently had six police districts, 25 police stations and 28 police posts, with 15 police personnel to be assigned to the TRX post on a rotational basis.

items.title: [UPDATED] PM: Govt to continue talks with US over 10pct tariff
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: The government will continue negotiations with the United States over a new 10 per cent tariff imposed on Malaysia, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said talks are now led by Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani, following initial efforts by his predecessor, Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. Earlier, US President Donald Trump's administration announced plans to impose new tariffs of 10 per cent and 12.5 per cent on goods from 60 trading partners, including Malaysia, effective today.

items.title: Dive association calls for probe into Pulau Tioman fatality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia Scuba Diving Association has called for a transparent investigation into a recent Pulau Tioman diving fatality, urging authorities and operators to strengthen safety standards nationwide. Its president Aminor Azmi Abdul Latip said relevant authorities and international dive training agencies should conduct a transparent review to determine the circumstances surrounding the incident and identify measures to prevent similar tragedies. He urged all dive operators nationwide to review and strengthen their safety procedures, emergency response plans and dive management practices.

items.title: Wholesale market blaze: Cause to be known in 14 days, says fire dept
items.summary: SELAYANG: The cause of the fire at the Selangor Wholesale Market is expected to be determined within 14 days, with the Fire and Rescue Department urging the public not to speculate while forensic investigations are under way. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the forensic investigation would begin once the site was declared safe and cleared of debris. "Usually, the forensic investigation takes around 14 days.

items.title: Rail upgrade from Gemas to Gua Musang completed
items.summary: JEMPOL: The government's rehabilitation and upgrading project for the east coast single-track railway line from Gemas, Negri Sembilan, to Gua Musang, Kelantan, has been completed, except for the remaining Gua Musang-Tumpat section. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the project was divided into three packages - Gemas-Mentakab (Package A), Mentakab-Gua Musang (Package B), and Gua Musang-Tumpat (Package C). When asked about the remaining sections of the railway rehabilitation project, Loke said in a press conference that Package C continued to face delays.

items.title: 3 Rohingya among 20 foreigners detained in Kelantan operation
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Three Rohingya with suspected fake United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cards were among 20 foreigners detained during a joint enforcement operation here today. Kelantan Immigration Department director Garry Belayong John Siki said the suspected fake UNHCR cards were seized from three Rohingya men during Op Mega, a coordinated operation involving the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) and the Kota Baru Municipal Council-Islamic City. He said the cards were confiscated after checks revealed discrepancies between the biometric data embedded in the cards and the physical appearance of the holders.

items.title: ‘Destined to sink’: Hannah Yeoh likens BN-PN alliance to the Titanic
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — DAP deputy secretary-general Hannah Yeoh has warned that the emerging cooperation between Barisan Nasional (BN) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) is destined to collapse, comparing the alliance to the doomed Titanic. “I likened their alliance to the ship in the film Titanic, a vessel destined to sink sooner or later,” Yeoh said, recounting her speech at a Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign event in Kuala Klawang, Jelebu. Yeoh was campaigning for incumbent Klawang assemblyman Datuk Bakri Sawir, who is seeking to retain the seat for PH.

items.title: HLB Private Bank expands access to global private markets
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: HLB Private Bank is broadening investment opportunities for Malaysian investors by offering eligible clients access to global private markets. This includes private equity and private credit strategies that have traditionally been available mainly to institutional investors. At its inaugural Private Markets Summit 2026 in Malaysia, the bank announced two upcoming investment offerings: the Global Private Equity Fund-of-Funds II and the Private Markets Evergreen Income Fund.

items.title: Johor police arrest three, seize 71 Bitcoin mining machines in TNB power theft bust
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 24 — Johor police have busted a syndicate illegally mining Bitcoin using electricity stolen from Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB), following four raids on July 22 and 23. Police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the operation, codenamed Ops Letrik, was carried out by the Johor Contingent Police Headquarters’ Criminal Investigation Department (D4), working alongside TNB’s Southern Region SEAL team. During a press conference at the Johor Contingent Police Headquarters today, he said the items seized included 71 cryptocurrency mining machines, two computers, two laptops, five routers, two monitors, two computer keyboards, a mobile phone and two vehicles.

items.title: Bangladeshi man claims trial to reckless driving charge over food rider's death
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: A Bangladeshi vegetable farm worker claimed trial at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of reckless driving that caused the death of a food delivery rider earlier this week. Md Habibullah, 28, pleaded not guilty to driving a Nissan Almera recklessly, resulting in the death of Muhammad Jafni Baharudin, 28. Deputy public prosecutor Alia Anisa Khairus Masnan did not offer bail and informed the court that the accused was not facing any immigration-related offences.

items.title: ‘Only Tok Min’: Loke says Aminuddin is PH’s sole Negeri Sembilan MB candidate
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 24 — Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) remains unanimous in backing Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun as its sole candidate for Menteri Besar should the coalition retain power in the state election, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said. Loke, who is also Negeri Sembilan DAP chairman, said no other candidate was being considered for the post besides Aminuddin, who also chairs Negeri Sembilan PH. “Our Menteri Besar candidate is only one person, that is Tok Min (Aminuddin).

items.title: Penang scrap goods trader charged with trafficking 4,625gm of heroin
items.summary: No plea was recorded from M. Davendran when the charge was read to him before magistrate S. Selvaranjini, as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. Deputy Public Prosecutor Lau Shavin prosecuted, while Davendran was represented by lawyer Farah Hamizah Hasan Gani. She also asked the court to fix a mention date pending the chemist's report.

items.title: AirAsia X confirms flight Kuala Lumpur-Bahrain-London Gatwick rescheduled to March 2027
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia X has rescheduled the launch of its Kuala Lumpur-Bahrain-London Gatwick services to March 2027, citing prolonged geopolitical instability in West Asia. AirAsia X said the decision followed a review of the operating environment, with safety and operational considerations remaining the airline's priority. The airline's general manager Benyamin Ismail said the carrier had made the difficult decision in view of the prolonged geopolitical instability in the region.

items.title: Pop, pop, pop: Worker recalls first moments of Selangor Wholesale Market blaze
items.summary: The store manager of a vegetable produce shop next to the section gutted by the blaze said the fire broke out at 10pm yesterday, barely an hour after he reported for work. "We heard popping sounds — 'pop, pop, pop'. By the time the fire department arrived, the fire had already reached this area," he said.

items.title: In food security push, Penang disburses RM3.28m in aid to rice and vegetable farmers
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, July 24 — The Penang state government is disbursing a one-off RM500 cash grant to 6,572 padi and vegetable farmers to alleviate the cost of living and bolster the state’s food supply chain. The aid will benefit 5,567 padi farmers and 1,005 vegetable growers across the state. Meanwhile, the state’s vegetable farmers produced 10,648 metric tonnes in 2025.

items.title: ‘Sepucuk Surat Sebuah Episod’ offers rare glimpse into letters from Malaysia’s prominent figures
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — In his latest book, Sepucuk Surat Sebuah Episod: Rakaman Pelaku-Pelaku Zaman , celebrated cultural figure Raja Ahmad Aminullah brings together personal letters exchanged with some of Malaysia’s key figures, offering a glimpse into the people who helped shape the nation’s story. Raja Ahmad, fondly known as Pak Engku, said the collection of personal correspondence and exclusive interactions was not intended as a political book, but as a socio-cultural documentation of his observations on the communities around him. The book was launched by the National Book Council of Malaysia (MBKM) director Mohd Khair Ngadiron, with the event attended by artists, literary figures, academics and Raja Ahmad’s long-time associates.

items.title: Police detain several after student and father attacked outside Serdang school
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Police have detained several individuals to assist investigations into the recent assault of a 16-year-old student and his father outside a school in Taman Desaminium, Serdang following the circulation of a video of the incident on social media. Serdang district police chief ACP Muhamad Farid Ahmad today said the incident occurred at about 12.45pm on July 3 when the student of Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Taman Desaminium was called over by several male students from the same school while walking towards his motorcycle outside the school compound. Prior to this, a video purportedly showing the incident, in which several secondary school students attacked a man at a motorcycle parking area near the school, went viral on social media, drawing widespread public attention.

items.title: ‘We can fix this’: BN touts itself as mediator for Negeri Sembilan royal dispute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Barisan Nasional (BN) has positioned itself as the key to resolving the ongoing royal dispute in Negeri Sembilan, claiming that a mandate to govern the state would allow the coalition to facilitate the best possible resolution between Yang Dipertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, and the state’s four Undang. Speaking on the KiniTV “Explain Sikit” podcast, state BN chief Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias urged voters to consider the state’s stability before casting their ballots on August 1. He argued that the current royal dispute has spiralled into a contentious deadlock that requires expert crisis management.

items.title: Police arrest man over attempted cash van robbery in Klang, recover stolen shotgun
items.summary: Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar said the suspect, in his 20s, was arrested in Shah Alam, Selangor, at about 11am yesterday. He said the suspect had allegedly attempted to rob a security van transporting cash before seizing a pump-action shotgun from a security guard and fleeing on a motorcycle. Kumar said checks showed the suspect had a previous record under Section 8 of the Arms Act 1960.

items.title: NGOs to protest at US Embassy over interference in Malaysia’s sovereign policies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Three non-governmental organisations (NGOs) will hold a peaceful assembly and submit a memorandum of protest to the United States (US) Embassy here today to oppose the attempt by US members of Congress to pressurise Malaysia over its sovereign policies. The three organisations are the Malaysian Consultative Council of Islamic Organisations (Mapim), the Muslim Youth Movement of Malaysia (ABIM) and Pertubuhan Kebajikan Rakyat Sejagat Malaysia (Sejagat). The gathering is themed “Rakyat Bersatu Tolak Campur Tangan AS” (The People United Against US Interference) and carries the slogan “Pertahankan Kedaulatan Negara” (Defend National Sovereignty).

items.title: Guan Eng moots MSME relief, education reform in letter to Anwar
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 24 — Former minister Lim Guan Eng has presented a strategic roadmap to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, outlining six critical interventions designed to safeguard the wellbeing of the people and drive Malaysia’s future economic progress. Underpinning the proposal is a call for urgent financial relief for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs). Beyond financial relief, Lim called for a digital overhaul of foreign worker processing.

items.title: Major fire erupts at Seri Kembangan wholesale market, gas tanks explode
items.summary: Selangor Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division Assistant Director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said that five nearby fire hydrants were unusable because they were under private management, forcing crews to rely entirely on water from fire engines and tanker lorries, BH reported. The fire, believed to have started at approximately 10:50 pm, spread rapidly due to strong winds. A massive contingent of 68 JBPM officers and 67 Fire Volunteer members were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Malaysian passport ranks world’s seventh most powerful, ahead of US, says index
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Malaysia’s passport remained the world’s seventh most powerful in the Henley Passport Index 2026, offering visa-free or visa-on-arrival access to 183 destinations. According to the index, Malaysia shared seventh place with Australia, Canada, Czechia, Latvia, New Zealand, Slovakia and Slovenia. Brunei was ranked 19th with access to 163 destinations.

items.title: New charges prompt removal of containers left up to 407 days at Sepanggar Port
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 24 — Firm action by the Sabah state government and implementation of new port charges have addressed the issue of abandoned containers at Sepanggar Port here, the Sabah State Legislative Assembly (DUN) was told yesterday. He added that the amendment also aims, among others, to harmonise the definition of ‘Minister’ under the Sabah Ports (Privatisation) Enactment 1998 with the Sabah Ports Authority Enactment 1981. Meanwhile, the sitting also passed the Sabah Native Affairs Council (Amendment) Bill 2026 and the District Chief, Native Chief and Headman Bill 2026, tabled by Sabah Local Government and Housing Minister Datuk Dr Mohd Arifin Mohd Arif.

items.title: ‘Never heard of it’: Sanusi shrugs off Hamzah’s ‘PBN’ label
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 24 — Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor has dismissed claims of any official discussion to formalise the electoral alliance between Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Barisan Nasional (BN) as “Perikatan Barisan Nasional” (PBN). The proposal, put forward by Parti Wawasan Negara President Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, was brushed aside by Sanusi, who also serves as PAS’s election director. There hasn’t even been any discussion at that level,” Sanusi told reporters during a PN and BN rally for the Jempol constituency yesterday.

items.title: Anwar: Indian community empowerment must focus on education, skills and jobs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Efforts to empower the Indian community must prioritise quality education, comprehensive welfare, skills development and better employment opportunities, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. In a Facebook post yesterday, Anwar said he emphasised these priorities during a meeting with Indian community leaders. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, urged the Malaysian Indian Transformation Unit (MITRA) and all related agencies to take a more proactive approach at the grassroots level.

items.title: Bumiputera petrol station operators urge review of credit card charges as fuel costs rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — The Bumiputera Petrol Station Operators Association of Malaysia (Bumipeda) has urged the government to review the Merchant Discount Rate (MDR) charged on credit card transactions. Its vice-president, Shahrul Nizam Md Radzi, said MDR charges are placing an increasing burden on petrol station operators, adding to the cost pressures they already face from rising fuel prices. “Therefore, sound policies should not only protect consumers but also ensure the entire supply chain remains sustainable,” he said.

items.title: Agricultural TVET graduate employment rises to 76pc, still below national average
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 24 — The employment rate of Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) graduates in agriculture under the Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (KPKM) increased from 61 per cent in 2018 to 76 per cent in 2024, but is still below the national TVET average of 95 per cent. The findings were contained in the Graduate Outcomes of Agricultural TVET in Malaysia report, which was presented at the presentation ceremony of the Agricultural TVET Study Report in Malaysia and KPKM Stakeholder Engagement Session on Food Security Aspects yesterday. “The study, which used KPKM data spanning several years, also analysed the income and entrepreneurial involvement of agricultural TVET graduates,” it said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Senate passes Bill extending Malaysia’s reach over child sexual offences committed overseas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — The Dewan Negara yesterday passed the Sexual Offences Against Children (Amendment) Bill 2026 to expand Malaysia’s legal jurisdiction over child sexual offences committed outside the country’s territory. Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said the amendment is important as child sexual crime cases involving foreign nationals in Malaysia recorded a 117 per cent increase over the five-year period from 2021 to 2025. Kulasegaran said the amendment was crucial to ensure Malaysia had sufficient jurisdiction to take action against perpetrators of child sexual offences who had a clear connection to Malaysia, even if the offences involved cross-border elements.

items.title: Advocacy group DHRRA urges reform after court denies citizenship to Malaysia-born children
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Civil society organisation DHRRA Malaysia has urged the government to find practical and compassionate solutions for children who remain non-citizens despite being raised solely by their Malaysian biological fathers. The call follows the Court of Appeal ruling that such children cannot automatically inherit their fathers' citizenship. While acknowledging the judicial boundary and the court's adherence to the Federal Constitution, DHRRA Malaysia stressed that policymakers reviewing citizenship laws must keep the best interests of the child as the primary consideration.

items.title: Fahmi backs release of Tabung Haji RCI report in name of transparency
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 24 — The final report of the Royal Commission of Inquiry (RCI) into Tabung Haji (TH) should be made public to enable depositors, contributors, and the wider public to gain a comprehensive understanding of past events, said Communications Minister Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil. He said releasing the report would uphold the principles of transparency and fairness while allowing the public to better understand the issues examined during the inquiry. Earlier, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government is prepared to release the final RCI report on Tabung Haji in due course to allow the public to know the findings of the inquiry into allegations surrounding the sale of the agency’s strategic assets.

items.title: MACC records statements from 10 witnesses over KWAP’s RM200m eFishery loss
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 24 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has recorded statements from 10 witnesses as part of its investigation into the RM200 million investment loss suffered by the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) in Indonesian aquaculture technology firm eFishery. Abd Halim said more individuals would be called to assist the investigation, including members of the Finance Committee and the KWAP Investment Panel involved in the investment. He said investigators were examining documents including investment proposal papers, due diligence reports, site visit reports, meeting minutes relating to eFishery, investment procedure documents and KWAP financial records.

items.title: Malaysia hit with new 10pc tariff as Trump rebuilds trade agenda
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Malaysia, among Asean countries, faces the lowest tariff of 10 per cent under the United States’ new Section 301 tariffs following alleged failure to impose and effectively enforce forced labour import rules. Apart from Malaysia, Cambodia and Indonesia are also among Asean countries that are facing 10 per cent duties. Greer said the USTR is taking final action at President Donald Trump’s direction, under Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, by imposing tariffs on 60 economies for their failure to impose and effectively enforce a prohibition on the importation of goods produced with forced labour.

items.title: Police arrest two bikers for ‘Superman’ rides on PLUS highway near Hulu Selangor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 24 — Two motorcyclists have been arrested after executing reckless “Superman” stunts on the North-South Expressway, which sparked outrage after the footage went viral on TikTok. The Hulu Selangor District Police Headquarters (IPD) was alerted to the incident on July 22 at approximately 4:06 am. Both riders were arrested and brought to the traffic police division at the Hulu Selangor District Police Headquarters for further questioning.

items.title: Sabah petitions federal government for transparent revenue declaration to secure 40pc entitlement
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 24 — Sabah has petitioned the federal government to require all companies operating within the state to separately declare revenue earned in Sabah, a move aimed at bolstering transparency in the negotiations over its constitutional 40 per cent revenue entitlement. “In those letters, among other things, we stressed the need for an official ruling to determine the method for declaring revenue and taxes by Sabah companies, as well as non-Sabah companies with operations in Sabah whose revenue is declared outside Sabah,” Masidi said. Chin proposed that Sabah implement a “collection at source” mechanism for revenue generated in the state.

items.title: A decade before a single brick is laid: Developer’s bold plan for Sabah’s Mengalum Island
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 24 — A Taiwanese developer says it will spend the next decade restoring coral reefs, protecting turtle nesting beaches and reforesting Sabah’s Mengalum Island before laying a single brick for what it hopes will become the world’s first net-zero carbon island resort. “We want to conserve and enhance the island before development,” Sinyi Realty Malaysia assistant general manager Henry Chou told delegates recently at the Sabah Asia-Pacific Impact Investing for Sustainable Development Summit. Located about 56km north-west of Kota Kinabalu, Mengalum Island is an uninhabited tropical island known for its white sandy beaches, crystal-clear waters and coral reefs.

items.title: Of ‘kain pelikat’ and ‘pagoda’ shirt: Ayob Khan jokes about opening mamak shop after retiring from PDRM this Sunday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Outgoing Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay joked that he could be seen serving customers at a mamak restaurant after retiring from the police force this Sunday. Asked about his plans after nearly 35 years with the Royal Malaysia Police, Ayob said he wanted to take a short break before deciding on his next move. “As I said earlier, I'll open a mamak restaurant.

items.title: From Roblox recruitment to personal grudges, Ayob Khan says extremist threats are changing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 23 — Militant and extremist groups in Malaysia are adapting their recruitment methods, with online platforms such as Roblox now being used to identify potential recruits, outgoing Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said today. He warned that the threat posed by such groups would never disappear, as the nature of radicalisation continues to change and authorities must stay ahead to prevent attacks. “The threat from militant and extremist groups will never end.

items.title: Flood walls, retention ponds, rent relief: Selangor’s RM40.5m response after latest PJ floods
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 23 — The Selangor state government is implementing the Immediate and Moderate Flood Mitigation Plan Project involving hotspot and high-risk areas under the administration of the Petaling Jaya City Council (MBPJ), with an allocation of RM40.5 million to reduce the risk of flash floods. State Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the allocation covers 39 scopes of work divided into immediate (quick win) and medium-term measures to address the flood issue in a more organised manner. Izham said that out of the total allocation, 26 projects were categorised under immediate measures involving a cost of RM20.1 million, while 13 other projects were under medium-term measures involving an allocation of RM20.4 million.
